title: Quiz: Transfer Learning Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of transfer learning concepts in QML

questions.question: What is the main goal of transfer learning?

options.text: Reuse knowledge from one task to improve another

questions.explanation: Transfer learning reuses knowledge (parameters, features) learned from a source task to improve performance on a target task, especially when target data is limited.

questions.question: In quantum transfer learning, which layers are typically frozen?

options.text: Early layers that learn general features

questions.explanation: Early layers typically learn general data encoding patterns that transfer well. These are frozen while later task-specific layers are fine-tuned.

questions.question: What does domain shift refer to?

options.text: Moving data to a different server
Difference in data distribution between source and target

questions.explanation: Domain shift is the difference in data distributions between source and target domains. Larger shifts make transfer more challenging.

questions.question: Transfer learning always improves performance on the target task.
questions.explanation: False. Negative transfer can occur when source and target domains are too different, actually hurting performance.

questions.question: What is Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) used for?

options.text: Measuring distance between source and target distributions

questions.explanation: MMD measures the distance between two probability distributions, helping quantify domain shift between source and target.

questions.question: When is transfer learning most beneficial?

options.text: When source and target domains are very different
When target data is limited and domains are similar
When training from scratch is fast

questions.explanation: Transfer learning provides the most benefit when target data is limited (can't train from scratch effectively) and domains are similar enough for knowledge to transfer.

questions.question: What is progressive unfreezing?

options.text: Unfreezing layers gradually from last to first

questions.explanation: Progressive unfreezing starts by training only the last layers, then gradually unfreezes earlier layers, allowing careful adaptation while preserving learned features.

questions.question: Why is transfer learning especially valuable for QML? (Select all)

options.text: Training data collection on quantum devices is costly
Pre-trained models can help avoid barren plateaus

questions.explanation: QML benefits from transfer learning because quantum resources are expensive, data collection is costly, and pre-trained initialization may help avoid barren plateaus. Quantum computers don't have unlimited capacity.
